body:
# Minimal Node.js HTTP server serving "Hello, World!" on `/`

## Context

We need a minimal HTTP server built on the Node.js standard library alone —
no Express, Fastify, Koa, or any other external web framework. The project is
already configured as an ESM package (`package.json` has `"type": "module"`),
so the server is authored with ESM `import` syntax using the built-in `node:http`
module.

Requirements the implementation must satisfy:

- Listen on the port given by the `PORT` environment variable, defaulting to
  `3000` when `PORT` is unset.
- A `GET` request to path `/` returns HTTP `200` with a `Content-Type` header
  of `text/html` and a response body containing the exact text
  `Hello, World!`.
- Expose an npm `start` script that launches the server.
- Keep the footprint to a single source file plus `package.json` — no extra
  modules, config files, or dependencies.

This is a self-contained capability with no dependency on other subsystems and
no external packages, so it fits a single standalone Story.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A single server source file exists (e.g. `server.js` at the repo root),
      authored in ESM syntax and importing only Node.js built-ins (`node:http`);
      the project has zero runtime dependencies added to `package.json`.
- [ ] The server binds to `process.env.PORT` when set and to `3000` when
      `PORT` is unset (verified by starting with and without `PORT`).
- [ ] A `GET /` request returns HTTP status `200`, a `Content-Type` response
      header whose value is `text/html` (charset suffix permitted, e.g.
      `text/html; charset=utf-8`), and a response body containing the exact
      substring `Hello, World!`.
- [ ] `package.json` defines a `"start"` script that launches the server via
      `node <server-file>`, and `npm start` boots the server successfully.
- [ ] The implementation is limited to one source file plus `package.json`
      (no additional source files, framework dependencies, or build step).

## Out of Scope

- Routing for any path other than `/` (a basic non-`/` fallback such as a 404
  is acceptable but not required by this Story).
- HTTPS/TLS termination, HTTP/2, or reverse-proxy configuration.
- Request logging, metrics, graceful-shutdown handling, or health-check
  endpoints beyond what the acceptance criteria require.
- Any external web framework or added npm dependency.
- Automated test tooling or CI wiring beyond the repo's existing scripts.

## Notes

- The repo's `package.json` already sets `"type": "module"`, so ESM `import`
  works without a rename to `.mjs`.
- Node's built-in `node:http` module is sufficient; `createServer` with a
  request handler that inspects `req.method` and `req.url` covers the `GET /`
  contract.
- Set the status code and `Content-Type` explicitly (`res.writeHead(200,
  { 'Content-Type': 'text/html' })`) so the response satisfies the exact
  header contract.
- Read the port once at startup: `const port = process.env.PORT || 3000;`.
title: Minimal Node.js HTTP server serving "Hello, World!" on `/`
